title: Cost of Equity: If a firm has a WACC of 0.08 and the...
summary: Apply the Capital Asset Pricing Model to calculate expected returns or evaluate security pricing.
prompt: If a firm has a WACC of 8% and the expected market return is 12%, what is the implied risk-free rate if the firm's beta is 1.5?

options.text: Slope of the security market line
Using the CAPM formula: Cost of equity = risk-free rate + beta × (market return

solution: Using the CAPM formula: Cost of equity = risk-free rate + beta × (market return - risk-free rate). Rearranging gives risk-free rate = (cost of equity - (beta × market return)) / (1 - beta). Plugging in values yields a risk-free rate of 5%.
